title: soul-forge profile
description: Structured facts about the human an agent fleet works with. Produced by an onboarding interview and consumed by `soul-forge import` + `soul-forge generate`. Every field is optional; capture what's known, leave the rest empty.

role.description: Job title or role, e.g. 'Staff Engineer'.

background.description: One or two lines of relevant background.

goals.description: What they're trying to achieve right now.

communication_style.description: How they like to communicate, e.g. 'direct, terse, examples over prose'.

expertise_areas.description: Domains they already know well — agents should skip the basics here.

learning_focus.description: Areas they're actively learning — agents should explain the 'why' here.

articulation.description: How precisely/tersely they phrase things — calibrates verbosity.

preferences.description: Things they value, e.g. 'minimal diffs', 'tests with the change'.

workflow.description: Their workflow, e.g. 'kanban with daily review'.

decision_style.description: How they decide, e.g. 'bias to action, adjust on feedback'.

feedback_style.description: How they give/receive feedback, e.g. 'blunt and direct'.

collab_style.description: Collaboration style, e.g. 'async-first, sync when stuck'.

languages.description: Programming or natural languages they use.

do_not_do.description: Hard 'do not' rules agents must treat as constraints.

output_preferences.description: Keyed output preferences, e.g. {'code': 'full blocks', 'explanations': 'short then example'}.

environment.description: Their machine and tooling. Never store secrets here.

updated_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp of the last update (optional).
